title: Building a hypersonic pathway to orbit with Mark Bigham
summary: The Space Minds podcast features a discussion with Mark Bigham, VP of Defense Programs at Longshot Space Technologies Corporation, about building a hypersonic pathway to orbit. Longshot Space Technologies is introduced as a new hardware startup in the space industry.

title: The ‘ground truth’ gap in AgTech: Why satellites alone can’t save supply chains
summary: Satellite technology is revolutionizing precision agriculture and supply chain monitoring in AgTech, but 'ground truth' data remains essential for accuracy. The article highlights the limitations of relying solely on satellites despite advancements in hardware, launch costs, and AI-driven analytics.

title: Industry says proposed NASA changes to commercial space station plans create confusion
summary: NASA's proposed changes to its support for commercial space stations have sparked concern and confusion among industry companies. The head of an industry organization warned about the potential impact of these changes.

title: SBQuantum and Spire to send quantum diamond magnetometer into orbit
summary: SBQuantum and Spire Global will launch a quantum diamond magnetometer into low-Earth orbit on March 30 aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rideshare. Spire will provide the satellite, ground stations, and data processing for the mission.

title: ispace redesigns lunar lander, introduces lunar communications service
summary: Japanese company ispace is redesigning its lunar lander and delaying its first mission by its American subsidiary. It has also announced plans for a lunar satellite constellation to provide communications services.

title: U.S. GSSAP satellites execute GEO handoff to monitor China’s Shijian-29 spacecraft
summary: U.S. GSSAP satellites performed a handoff maneuver to closely monitor China's Shijian-29 spacecraft in geostationary orbit. The operation involved coordinated tracking to maintain proximity and continuous observation.

title: Space Command begins phased move to Alabama
summary: The U.S. Space Command has initiated a phased relocation to Alabama, with the new headquarters expected to be completed by 2031.

title: Government use of commercial procurement models has limitations in space
summary: Government agencies are adopting commercial procurement models for space capabilities, but this does not guarantee more business opportunities for companies. The shift highlights potential limitations in the commercialization of government space contracts.

title: Turning growth into profits remains a challenge as space demand grows
summary: The space industry is experiencing growth across multiple sectors, but turning this demand into sustainable profits remains a challenge. Questions persist about the long-term financial viability of emerging opportunities.

title: Xona raises $170 million for satellite navigation network
summary: Xona secures $170 million in funding to expand its satellite navigation network, aiming to deploy a 258-satellite constellation for enhanced positioning, navigation, and timing services.

title: 'For All Mankind' season 5: Release date & how to watch Apple TV's alt-history space show
summary: Apple TV+ has announced the release date for 'For All Mankind' season 5, the alt-history space show. The fifth season will premiere on February 28, 2024.

title: New portrait of the oldest-known supernova | Space photo of the day for March 27, 2026
summary: A NASA X-ray telescope captured a detailed portrait of the oldest-known supernova remnant, revealing previously unseen features of the exploded star. This image was featured as Space Photo of the Day on March 27, 2026.

title: NASA's ambitious 'decade of Venus' exploration may bank on 1 probe: 'Not everything can move forward'
summary: NASA may scale back its 'decade of Venus' exploration plans due to budget constraints, potentially limiting future missions to just one probe. The agency faces tough decisions across its planetary science portfolio to manage financial pressures.

title: What time is NASA's Artemis 2 moon launch on April 1?
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission, the first crewed moon flight in over 50 years, is scheduled to launch on April 1. The event will be broadcast live for public viewing.

title: NASA is developing the '1st nuclear powered interplanetary spacecraft.' What about the Voyager probes?
summary: NASA is developing its first nuclear-powered interplanetary spacecraft. The article explains nuclear electric propulsion and distinguishes it from radioisotope thermoelectric generators (RTGs) used by the Voyager probes.

title: Watch the BBC's Dr Who spin-off 'The War Between the Land and the Sea' and every episode of the classic Dr Who series on your travels with our exclusive NordVPN deal
summary: The news mentions a BBC Dr Who spin-off titled 'The War Between the Land and the Sea' and promotes a NordVPN deal for streaming the series. The promotion highlights watching the UNIT defend humanity against the Sea Devils.

title: Hitting the brakes: Hubble Space Telescope watches doomed comet reverse its spin
summary: The Hubble Space Telescope observed comet 41P/Tuttle–Giacobini–Kresák's rotation slowing, reversing, and then speeding up, with jet-like gas outbursts likely causing the changes.

title: Sun storms are powered by a magnetic engine 16 Earths deep, study finds
summary: A study reveals that the sun's powerful magnetic field, which drives space storms, originates about 16 Earths deep beneath its surface. This discovery challenges previous assumptions about the sun's magnetic engine.

title: 'For All Mankind' is confirmed for season 6, but it'll be the final mission for Apple TV's incredible space show
summary: Apple TV+ has confirmed a sixth season for the space drama 'For All Mankind', which will serve as the final installment of the series.

title: Don't miss the waxing moon swing by the Beehive star cluster March 27
summary: The moon will pass by the Beehive star cluster on March 27-28, an event visible to stargazers. This celestial alignment involves the moon and over 1,000 stars in the cluster.

title: Satellite Spots a Spawn
summary: Satellite imagery detected the activity of herring around Vancouver Island, which brightened coastal waters enough to be visible from space. This observation highlights the use of satellite technology in monitoring marine ecosystems.

title: What’s Up: April 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
summary: NASA shares skywatching tips for April 2026, highlighting Mercury's brightness, the Lyrid meteor shower peak, and a new comet's appearance.

title: La NASA presentará el telescopio Roman completo y ofrecerá una conferencia de prensa
summary: NASA will unveil the completed Roman Space Telescope and hold a press conference on April 21, showcasing the spacecraft ahead of its upcoming launch.

title: NASA to Unveil Complete Roman Telescope, Host Media Briefing
summary: NASA is set to unveil the completed Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, which has finished construction and is undergoing final prelaunch testing. The media briefing will provide a last chance to view the fully integrated telescope before it is shipped for launch.

title: NASA Releases Artemis II Moon Mission Launch Countdown
summary: NASA has announced the launch countdown for the Artemis II Moon mission, which will send four astronauts around the Moon. The countdown will begin approximately two days before liftoff.

title: Station Nation: Aaron Rose, Cold Stowage Mission Manager
summary: Aaron Rose, a Cold Stowage Mission Manager, supports critical cargo resupply missions to the International Space Station by coordinating temperature-controlled science experiments. His team ensures all cold stowage hardware, operations, and personnel are properly managed for safe transport.

title: Arctic Winter Sea Ice Ties Record Low, NASA, NSIDC Scientists Find
summary: Arctic winter sea ice tied the record low for the second consecutive year, with a peak of 5.52 million square miles on March 15, matching the lowest level observed since satellite monitoring began in 1979.

title: NASA’s IXPE Gets Fresh Look at Supernova
summary: NASA's IXPE mission captured a new observation of the supernova RCW 86, enhancing data from other X-ray telescopes. The image, released in March 2026, provides a more complete view of the supernova.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4838-4844: Wrapping Up the Boxwork Terrain
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover has completed a series of intensive science observations and engineering activities while investigating the Martian boxwork terrain. The rover's monthslong study of this geological feature has now concluded.

title: La NASA anuncia la cobertura de la de la primera misión tripulada Artemis alrededor de la Luna
summary: NASA announces coverage for the first crewed Artemis mission around the Moon, including pre-launch, launch, and mission events. The target date is set for no earlier than April 1, with a two-hour launch window.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon Base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Lunar Gateway. This marks a significant change in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket. The company, based in Ottobrunn, Germany, had planned to proceed with the launch but faced an issue that led to the cancellation.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, targeting a 2028 launch. Administrator Jared Isaacman and NASA leaders revealed the plans.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is accelerating ground work at Starbase in preparation for upcoming Block 3 Starship launches, highlighting efforts to increase launch cadence.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the U.S. will launch internet satellites using Falcon 9 and Atlas V. The launches are scheduled across multiple countries including the United States, Russia, China, New Zealand, and Norway.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 launched to the International Space Station (ISS) from a repaired launch pad at Baikonur Cosmodrome. The mission follows repairs made after the Soyuz MS-28 launch from the same site.

title: Blue Origin ramps up New Glenn manufacturing, unveils Orbital Data Center ambitions
summary: Blue Origin is increasing the manufacturing pace of its New Glenn heavy-lift rocket and has announced plans to develop an Orbital Data Center. This marks a significant expansion in the company's space infrastructure ambitions.

title: SLS enters pad flow ahead of historic Artemis II mission
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft have arrived at the launch pad in preparation for the upcoming Artemis II mission. This marks a significant milestone ahead of the historic lunar mission.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2 in preparation for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12. This marks another milestone toward the next launch attempt.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: Nine launches are scheduled for this week, including missions by Falcon 9, Spectrum, and Electron rockets. This marks a busy period for space launches across different providers.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet celebrating 50 hallmark achievements over its 50-year history. This publication highlights key milestones and contributions in space exploration and technology.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after losing communication a month ago due to an anomaly. The mission team confirmed the spacecraft is operational and has 'phoned home'.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to discuss the implementation of decisions from the CM25 ministerial meeting, focusing on industrial opportunities and thematic priorities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in ESA's Large Space Simulator. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is expected to launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, mandating its use by all suppliers for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a compact, 3D-printed lens antenna for satellite navigation that is resistant to interference and cost-effective to produce. The football-sized antenna offers a lightweight alternative to bulky and expensive traditional solutions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly onboard Proba-3's Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have begun experiments using ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments. This initiative aims to support educational and research activities in space science.

title: Xona Space Systems Raises $170M Series C to Scale Private GPS
summary: Xona Space Systems has raised $170 million in a Series C funding round to expand its private GPS technology. The company aims to provide stronger, more accurate, and secure PNT signals in LEO compared to traditional GPS.

title: Jared Isaacman Hits 100 Days In Office With a Bang
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman marks 100 days in office with a bold announcement about returning to the Moon. The statement suggests renewed focus on lunar missions under his leadership.

title: With Artemis Changes, Europe is Left Holding the Bag
summary: ESA and European space primes invested heavily in components for the lunar orbiting station under Artemis plans, but recent changes have left them in a difficult position. Europe now faces uncertainty regarding its role in the Artemis program.

title: Arkadia Space Secures €14.5M to Develop New Biprop Engine
summary: Arkadia Space, a Spanish propulsion startup, secured €14.5 million from the European Investment Council to develop a new bipropellant engine.

title: Lawmakers Question NASA’s Shifting Vision for CLDs
summary: Lawmakers are questioning NASA's changing vision for Commercial Low Earth Orbit Destinations (CLDs), as proposed by Administrator Jared Isaacman. The new plan has left many in Congress confused about the future of America's human presence in low Earth orbit.

title: Directed Energy: An Alternative to Space-Based Interceptors
summary: The article discusses directed energy as an alternative to space-based interceptors (SBIs) for space defense. It highlights the potential of directed energy systems in addressing space threats.

title: Commerce Unveils One-Stop Licensing Proposal For Novel Space Missions
summary: The U.S. Department of Commerce introduced a one-stop licensing proposal for novel space missions, aiming to streamline regulatory authority amid ongoing debates over mission authorization frameworks.

title: PAVE Space Raises $40M for In-Space Propulsion Tech
summary: PAVE Space has secured $40 million in funding to advance its in-space propulsion technology, which uses storable bipropellants to simplify and reduce the cost of launch operations compared to cryogenic fuels.

title: Airbase Emerges From Stealth With $5M to Automate Spectrum Allocation
summary: Airbase, a new startup, has emerged from stealth mode with $5 million in funding to automate spectrum allocation for the growing number of space missions. The company aims to address the increasing demand for spectrum management as orbital activities expand.

title: NASA Priorities Get a Major Makeover
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman has introduced a new plan to shift NASA's priorities towards a commercially driven lunar economy with surface infrastructure. This marks a significant change in NASA's strategic direction.

title: Bridgit Mendler Outlines Northwood’s Vision for Rapidly Deployable Ground Infrastructure
summary: Bridgit Mendler discussed Northwood Space's efforts to address satellite ground infrastructure challenges by creating an end-to-end ground system designed for rapid deployment. The initiative aims to streamline satellite operations and improve efficiency in the space industry.

title: Interview With AvL CTO Chris Rose at SATShow Week
summary: AvL Technologies CTO Chris Rose discusses the evolution of tactical fly-away systems during an interview at SATShow Week. The conversation highlights advancements in the field from AvL's booth.

title: D2D, Sovereign Demand, and Mega-Valuations: Space Leaders Navigate Market Volatility
summary: Space industry leaders at SATShow expressed uncertainty about the market's future, with some acknowledging current volatility. The discussion focused on direct-to-direct (D2D) strategies, sovereign demand, and high valuations.

title: Interview With Qorvo Director of Satcom & Systems Engineering Ryan Jennings at SATShow Week
summary: Qorvo's Director of Satcom & Systems Engineering Ryan Jennings discusses the company's RF solutions at SATShow Week, including phased arrays for LEO satellites.

title: Sovereign Satcom Networks Grapple With Data Security Amid Geopolitical Uncertainty
summary: Nations are prioritizing sovereign control over their satellite communications networks due to geopolitical uncertainties. The challenge lies in defining the scope and capabilities of sovereign space infrastructure.

title: Investors Debate IPO Path, Financing Routes Needed to Grow Space Market
summary: Investors are discussing the need for better financing routes, such as IPOs, to support the growth of the commercial space sector. Venture capitalists at a SATShow panel highlighted that financing has not kept pace with the industry's technological advancements.

title: Interview With Kepler Chief Revenue Officer Beau Jarvis at SATShow Week
summary: Kepler Communications' Chief Revenue Officer Beau Jarvis discusses the company's strategy for downlinking data from space to Earth during SATShow Week. The interview highlights Kepler's approach to addressing this key challenge in the space industry.

title: The Evolving Influence of IFC Driving Network Development
summary: Airlines have adopted inflight connectivity (IFC) as a standard offering for passengers over the past two decades. IFC developers are now expanding their efforts to meet growing demand.

title: Government Investment, Regulations Are the Key Trends to Watch in India, APAC Space
summary: Government investment and regulations are identified as key trends driving the growth of the space economy in India and the Asia-Pacific region. The role of governments as investors and market creators is emphasized.

title: Interview With Comtech Satellite & Space President Daniel Gizinski at SATShow Week
summary: Comtech Satellite & Space President Daniel Gizinski discusses the first delivery of Comtech’s Digital Common Ground 7000 series modems and live multi-orbit demonstrations at SATShow Week.
